content_class_declaration: nav/planning — audit + component-API design; measured by API fitness + duplication-targeted, not reduction-%

step_1_capture_state.target_surface: 5 role-landing pages + their typed data modules + DocumentationLayout slot structure
step_1_capture_state.method: static read of all 5 pages + DocumentationLayout (159 ln) + compliance/enterprise data modules; line inventory; duplication scan

pre_change_inventory.shared_structure:
All 5 pages: frontmatter imports DocumentationLayout + CardGrid + buildTechArticleJsonLD + their page data module; build jsonLD via buildTechArticleJsonLD({title, description, url: Astro.url.href}); render with

then

sections + CardGrid(s); close with a 'Next Steps' CardGrid.

duplication_confirmed.jsonLD_boilerplate: Identical import { buildTechArticleJsonLD } from '../../utils/seo' + the const jsonLD = buildTechArticleJsonLD({ title, description, url: Astro.url.href }) call in ALL 5 pages. The title/description args differ per page (and sometimes differ from the DocumentationLayout title/description — e.g. educators jsonLD title 'aDNA Teaching Kit' matches H1; startup-first-hour jsonLD description differs slightly from layout description). So the call must move into the component WITH optional overrides to preserve byte-for-byte SEO.
duplication_confirmed.lede_css_rule: Identical 4-property rule { font-size: var(--text-lg); line-height: 1.6; color: var(--color-text); margin-bottom: var(--space-8); } in the local <style> of ALL 5 pages. Byte-identical -> safe to centralize once in the component.

not_duplication_keep_local.note: Single-use styles/markup stay in their one page; centralizing them would be over-abstraction (Risk #1).

pre_change_inventory.layout_structure:
DocumentationLayout wraps inside
, after rendering Breadcrumb + optional heroImage +

{title}

; PrevNextNav after the slot. Astro scopes a page's

step_2_persona_qa.method: agent-simulated 4-persona Q&A on the consolidation approach (D15 allocation per m50_decadal_theme_set.md)

q1_first_impression.question: Do the 5 role pages read as one family or five one-offs?
q1_first_impression.answer: Mechanically near-identical chrome (same layout, same lede, same jsonLD pattern, same Next-Steps footer) but maintained as 5 copies. A shared component makes the family explicit and keeps them from drifting apart.

q2_clarity.question: Will a shared component obscure each page's distinct teaching content?
q2_clarity.answer: Not if it is a thin wrapper with a default body slot. Keep each page's distinct middle (educators' 3-week arc, compliance's 5 questions); share only the chrome.

q3_conciseness.question: Is there real boilerplate to cut, or is this churn?
q3_conciseness.answer: Real: every page repeats the jsonLD call + the .lede rule. Cutting it is a genuine DRY win that also prevents the SEO/style drift already visible between pages. The per-page body stays.

q4_anti_pattern.question: Risk of over-abstraction forcing distinct pages into one rigid mold?
q4_anti_pattern.answer: Yes if props-everything. Avoid by slotting the body and only owning genuinely-shared chrome (lede + jsonLD). Leave single-use classes (.eval-table) local. Prove parity on the most complex page (enterprise) before migrating the simpler four.

pre_change_dimension_aggregate_scores.note: planning cycle scored on design fitness, not page reduction. 4/4 consensus: thin-wrapper-over-DocumentationLayout; component owns lede (named slot) + jsonLD (with overrides); body via default slot; single-use styles stay local; enterprise migrates first as parity proof.

step_3_decide.change_selected: Design the <PersonaPage> API; build deferred to cycle 132 (one-change-per-cycle).

props.title: string (required) — DocumentationLayout title + H1
props.jsonLDTitle: string (optional, defaults to title) — TechArticle jsonLD title; override when a page's SEO title differs from its H1
props.jsonLDDescription: string (optional, defaults to description) — TechArticle jsonLD description; override to preserve byte-for-byte SEO where it differs from the layout description

component_api_design.internal: const jsonLD = buildTechArticleJsonLD({ title: jsonLDTitle ?? title, description: jsonLDDescription ?? description, url: Astro.url.href }); — removes the import+call from all 5 pages

slots.lede:
named slot — rendered as

...

inside the component so the scoped .lede style applies
slots.default: page body — distinct middle: sections, CardGrids, lists, tables

component_api_design.does_not_own: .eval-table (enterprise local) + any single-use page markup/styles + CardGrid (already shared) + the page data imports
component_api_design.page_usage_shape: import PersonaPage from '../../components/sections/PersonaPage.astro'; import CardGrid + data module as before; <PersonaPage title=... description=... jsonLDTitle=... jsonLDDescription=...><Fragment slot='lede'>...</Fragment> ...body... </PersonaPage>
component_api_design.scope_note: The lede MUST be passed via the named 'lede' slot (rendered inside PersonaPage), NOT as default-slot markup, or PersonaPage's scoped .lede will not match it (Risk #7).

step_4_implement.implementation_notes: Planning cycle — no code emitted (one-change-per-cycle; build is cycle 132). Deliverable is the audit inventory + the <PersonaPage> API design recorded in step_3.

step_5_re_measure.method: design review against the 4-persona consensus + the over-abstraction guardrail (Risk #1) + the scoped-style constraint (Risk #7)

post_change_inventory.over_abstraction_guardrail: thin wrapper + default body slot; single-use classes left local; enterprise-first parity proof
post_change_inventory.seo_parity_mechanism: jsonLDTitle/jsonLDDescription optional overrides preserve per-page SEO byte-for-byte

step_6_validate.method: 5-lens continuous-discipline overlay (applied to the design + spec prose; no page prose edited this cycle)

five_lens.lens_1_word_count_outliers: n/a — planning cycle; no rendered prose edited
five_lens.lens_4_clarity_checklist: pass — API is one-concept-per-prop; the single non-obvious behavior (Astro scoped-style + slot) is flagged in scope_note + Risk #7
five_lens.lens_5_reference_collection_scan: n/a — design recorded in the cycle JSON (correct collection)

post_change_dimension_scores.note: design validated; cleared for cycle 132 (build component + migrate enterprise reference page + build green)

step_7_record.iii_result: Cycle 131 complete. Audit confirmed real line counts 75/89/135/123/116 = 538 and the duplication target = 5x buildTechArticleJsonLD boilerplate + 5x byte-identical .lede rule. <PersonaPage> API designed: thin wrapper over DocumentationLayout that builds jsonLD internally (with jsonLDTitle/jsonLDDescription overrides for byte-for-byte SEO parity), owns .lede, renders the lede via a named slot, and passes the body via the default slot. Enterprise migrates first (cycle 132) as the parity proof.

lift_honored: typed-data-module pattern preserved (page data imports unchanged)

step_7_record.self_reference_so8: 5 role pages collapsing onto 1 reusable primitive demonstrates aDNA's module->lattice composition discipline on the vault's own site — the structure is the lesson.
seeds: skill_thin_wrapper_component_extraction (1/3) — extract shared chrome via a thin wrapper + default slot, leaving single-use styles local
skill_astro_scoped_style_slot_constraint (1/3) — render shared styled elements inside the component (named slot), never via default slot, so scoped CSS applies
skill_seo_parity_override_props (1/3) — when centralizing per-page SEO/jsonLD, expose optional override props so consolidation preserves byte-for-byte metadata
